data.title: 22nd Century outlines plans for VLN expansion and growth in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: 22nd Century Group (XXII) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Larry Firestone emphasized the company's transformation over the past year, highlighting the focus on restructuring and the divestment of the hemp/cannabis business to become a pure-play tobacco company. He stated that "2025 is essentially the launch of the new 22nd Century," driven by a rebranded VLN product and new partner VLN SKUs.

  • The company has secured a long-term commitment with Smoker Friendly and is working with other CMO customers to stabilize revenues and improve contract terms. Firestone noted the importance of "rate of sale and carton volume" to drive growth in 2025.

  • CFO Dan Otto reported progress in reducing liabilities by $18 million in 2024 and further reducing debt in Q1 2025 by $3.1 million. He stated, "We are poised for revenue growth and margin improvement with our CMO customer contracts in 2025 and will be rapidly expanding VLN points of distribution during the year."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its goal to achieve breakeven EBITDA by Q4 2025. Firestone highlighted the "gradual walk-up" approach in rolling out the VLN product to ensure strong customer awareness and rate of sale.

  • The company aims to complete state regulatory approvals for VLN sales across all 50 states by July 2025. International expansion opportunities are also being explored, with discussions underway with potential partners.

  • The FDA’s proposed nicotine content rule provides a potential tailwind for VLN, as it is the only FDA-authorized product meeting the proposed standards.

Financial Results

  • The company reported Q4 2024 net revenue of $4 million, down from $5.9 million in Q3 2024, attributed to restructuring of CMO contracts and lower filtered cigar volumes. Gross margin was a loss of $1.2 million.

  • Operating expenses remained flat at $2.8 million compared to Q3 2024, reflecting cost-containment measures.

  • Year-over-year, 22nd Century reduced total liabilities by nearly 50% to $17.7 million and improved net working capital to $1.6 million from a deficit of $8.8 million.

Q&A

  • Andrew White, Emerging Growth Research: Questioned whether CMO contract terminations were specific to 2024. Firestone confirmed that "the reshuffling of the CMO contracts has gone full term," with new agreements already in production.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts appeared cautiously optimistic, focusing on the timeline for achieving breakeven EBITDA and the execution of VLN’s rollout strategy.

  • Management showed confidence in their strategic direction, with Firestone stating, "Our posture is predominantly offensive versus defensive as we book new contracts and lay the foundation for the future."

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Current quarter’s emphasis shifted heavily toward the VLN product rollout and strategic realignment. Compared to Q3 2022, where discussions revolved around the hemp/cannabis segment and initial VLN rollout, the focus is now squarely on tobacco.

  • Positive sentiment from management has strengthened, with explicit goals for profitability and market penetration outlined for 2025.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing cash burn and operating losses as challenges. Firestone stressed the need for "outside investment support" for funding.

  • Dependence on regulatory approvals and consumer adoption of VLN products remain critical risks.

Final Takeaway

The company has made significant progress in restructuring and positioning itself as a focused tobacco player, with its VLN product at the core of future growth. Management’s emphasis on disciplined execution and strategic partnerships underscores their commitment to achieving breakeven EBITDA by Q4 2025 while expanding the VLN product line domestically and internationally.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: IceCure tumbles after updating plans to seek FDA nod for ProSense
data.description:
• Shares of IceCure Medical (NASDAQ:ICCM) fell in the morning hours Thursday after the Israeli medical device maker updated investors on its plan to secure FDA clearance for its cancer treatment device, ProSense.
  • Commenting on its plans to launch ProSense in the U.S., IceCure Medical (NASDAQ:ICCM) said the FDA is expected to issue a de novo marketing authorization for the device after Q1 2025, targeting patients with early-stage, low-risk breast cancer receiving endocrine therapy.
  • In a letter to shareholders early this year, ICCM CEO Eyal Shamir announced a potential FDA marketing authorization for ProSense in Q1 2025.
  • The minimally invasive cryoablation system designed to kill tumor cells by freezing was endorsed by an FDA advisory committee panel in November. The FDA AdCom panels issue non-binding recommendations that the agency considers before authorizing medical products.

data.title: Trump’s Tariffs: A timeline of the ongoing global trade tension
data.description:
Ever since U.S. President Donald Trump has taken office, one of the many things that constantly dominated headlines was his tariff policy.

Although Trump had imposed tariffs during his first term, his latest actions roiled markets, disrupted diplomatic ties and shook industries.

The back-and-forth tariff stance and retaliatory measures has put investors on shaky ground. Major stock indexes plunged due to uncertainty and deteriorating sentiment, mostly fuelled by concerns stemming from worries of a looming trade war.

The S&P500 (SP500) dropped over 3% since the beginning of the year, while Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) declined over 7% and 1% respectively.

Here is a timeline of President Trump’s constantly changing tariff decisions so far:

Jan. 20, 2025

Hours after President Trump was sworn in, he announced 25% tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico starting on Feb. 1 and gave hints of a stricter tariffs on China.

"We're thinking in terms of 25 percent on Mexico and Canada, because they're allowing vast numbers of people -- Canada's a very bad abuser also -- vast numbers of people to come in, and fentanyl to come in," he said in the Oval Office.

Jan 22, 2025

After announcing his plan to charge 25% tariff on Canada and Mexico, Trump said he is now planning to levy 10% tariff on imports from China.

“We're talking about a tariff of 10 percent on China based on the fact that they're sending fentanyl to Mexico and Canada,” Trump told reporters. “Probably Feb. 1 is the date we’re looking at,” he added.

President Trump also threatened Russia with tariffs and sanctions if it did not reach an agreement to end the war in Ukraine.

Jan. 31, 2025

The White House said President Donald Trump's proposed 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico and 10% tariff on China will indeed go into effect on February 1.

Feb. 2, 2025

Canada Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada will place 25% tariffs on $155 billion in U.S. imports in retaliation for those tariffs enacted by President Trump.

Trudeau said his country's tariffs would be "far-reaching," including levies on beer, wine, bourbon, fruits and fruit juices including orange juice, vegetables, perfumes, clothing and shoes, according to the BBC.

Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum also said the country is looking to counter-tariffs as well.

Feb. 3, 2025

The Trump administration will delay imposing tariffs on Mexican goods imported into the U.S. after Mexico on Monday said it will rush 10,000 members of its National Guard to reinforce the border to prevent drug trafficking at the border shared by the countries.

Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau also said the U.S. will pause its plan to impose tariffs on Canada, outlining an agreement to bolster border supervision.

Feb. 7, 2025

Trump said he would announce plans for "reciprocal tariffs".

"If India, China, or any other country hits us with a 100 or 200 percent tariff on American-made goods, we will hit them with the same exact tariff. In other words, 100 percent is 100 percent. If they charge US, we charge THEM—an eye for an eye, a tariff for a tariff, same exact amount,” Trump had explained.

Feb. 10, 2025

President Trump signed the executive orders after-hours Monday imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S., canceling all exemptions and duty-free quotas for major suppliers Canada, Mexico, Brazil and other countries.

Feb. 11, 2025

The European Union vows to respond to U.S. tariffs with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen saying “unjustified tariffs on the EU will not go unanswered and will trigger firm and proportionate countermeasures.”

Feb. 13, 2025

Trump signed a memorandum that could result in sweeping reciprocal tariffs against all American trading partners.

"Under the Plan, my Administration will work strenuously to counter non-reciprocal trading arrangements with trading partners by determining the equivalent of a reciprocal tariff with respect to each foreign trading partner," the White House said.

Feb. 18, 2025

Trump said he would probably announce 25% tariffs on imports of cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals on April 2.

Feb. 24, 2025

Trump on Monday said tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico “will go forward” on time after a month-long pause expires.

Feb. 27, 2025

Trump said his plan to impose tariffs on Mexican and Canadian goods will go into effect in March 4, and said another additional 10% levy will be placed on products from China.

He said he’s moving forward with the levies because drugs are “still pouring into” the U.S. from Mexico and Canada at “very high and unacceptable levels.” 

Feb. 28, 2025

Mexico has proposed matching tariffs that the U.S. has on Chinese goods, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said.

March 3, 2025

Trump said his proposal for reciprocal tariffs will go into effect on April 2. Separately, he said tariffs on “external” agricultural products will begin April 2. He didn't specify a tariff level in a post on his Truth Social site.

March 4, 2025

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said President Trump is likely to announce a tariff-relief deal for Mexico and Canada.

March 5, 2025

The White House said President Trump has granted automakers Ford (F), General Motors (GM), and Stellantis NV (STLA) a one-month reprieve on tariffs just imposed by the U.S. on Mexico and Canada.

March 6, 2025

Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said he will continue to impose tariffs on U.S. imports into the country until U.S. President Donald Trump cancels levies on Canadian products.

Separately, press reports suggested that the U.S. will delay tariffs on certain Canadian goods until early April.

March 7, 2025

President Trump said he's "strongly considering" large-scale banking sanctions, tariffs, and other sanctions on Russia until a cease fire and final settlement agreement is reached in the Russia-Ukraine war.  He also suggested that Canada may be hit with reciprocal tariffs on its dairy and lumber products soon.

March 11, 2025

Trump announced he would impose an additional 25% tariff on all Canadian steel and aluminum imports effective March 12. He said the move was in retaliation to the Ontario government's implementation of a 25% surcharge on all electricity exports to the U.S.

Following this, Ontario said it would suspend its 25% surcharge on electricity exports to the U.S. after agreeing to discuss a renewed trade agreement.

March 12, 2025

Trump has halted a plan to double tariffs on imports of Canadian steel and metal to 50%, just hours after announcing them

March 13, 2025

President Trump threatened to implement a 200% tariff on imports of all alcoholic beverages from the European Union. This comes after the EU two days ago announced its countermeasures and said it would target €26B worth of U.S. export goods, including products such as whisky.

March 20, 2025

President Trump said India will be hit by reciprocal tariffs from April 2, even though according to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, India is looking to reduce tariff and non-tariff barriers with the U.S. and strengthen trade in the goods and services sector through the multi-sector Bilateral Trade Agreement that is under the works.

Meanwhile, EU countermeasures to U.S. tariffs, are likely to take effect in mid-April, European Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic said.

 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Unilever added as a new long idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
Unilever (NYSE:UL) was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye with the potential for 33% upside for the consumer packaged goods company. "The CEO change is a positive catalyst empowering an agent of growth, and the signs of a soft first quarter were prophetic as more companies are providing Q1 updates," Hedgeye analyst Daniel Biolsi wrote in a note on Thursday.

The upcoming spin-off of Unilever's (NYSE:UL) ice cream business should help make "steady growth" in the U.S. more apparent, and other divestments are expected to be made. 

"Market share is improving in Europe, particularly in the Personal Care and Home Care segments," Biolsi added. "Early signs of a pivot point to a bottoming process in the Asia, Pacific, and Africa regions. The company could be the next large cap re-rating in CPG, with leading peers trading at a 50% premium."

Unilever (UL) ADRs ticked higher by 0.2% on Thursday.

data.title: Klarna to be offered as DoorDash payment option
data.description:
Klarna (KLAR) and DoorDash (DASH) have teamed up to offer Klarna's flexible payment options to DoorDash customers, the companies said on Thursday.

In coming months, DoorDash (DASH) customers will be able to choose from Klarna's range of payment options when buying groceries, retail, or the DashPass Annual Plan on DoorDash's website or app.

Payment options will include:

  • Paying in full;
  • Pay in four equal interest-free installments; or
  • Pay Later, by choosing a date that aligns with the customer's paycheck schedule.

"Our partnership with DoorDash marks an important milestone in Klarna’s expansion into everyday spending categories,"  said David Sykes, chief commercial officer at Klarna (KLAR).

data.description:

data.title: Eyenovia falls on plans to seek reverse merger
data.description:
Shares of Eyenovia (NASDAQ:EYEN) traded lower on Thursday after the ophthalmic product developer announced a non-binding agreement to seek a reverse merger with Betaliq, Inc., a privately held pharmaceutical company based in Florida.

Per the terms of a non-binding letter of intent the company has signed, Betaliq equity holders are set to own about 84% of the combined entity, while Eyenovia (NASDAQ:EYEN) will own the rest.

The transaction is set to create a new publicly listed eye care company, valuing the merging parties at approximately $77M and $15M, respectively.

Eyenovia (NASDAQ:EYEN), a nano-cap pharmaceutical firm with a market capitalization of roughly $3.5M, fell ~18% in the morning hours after the announcement.

In January, the developer of the Optejet drug delivery platform announced plans to evaluate strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value.

The proposed transaction combining the Optejet platform with Betaliq’s EyeSol drug delivery technology “is in the best interests of our company, our team members, patients, and shareholders,” said Michael Rowe, EYEN CEO.

data.title: Instacart launches new AI tools for advertisers to drive growth for brands
data.description:
Online grocery delivery platform Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) on Thursday introduced a suite of new AI-powered automation tools that would help advertisers boost campaign performance for brands.

The new tools include AI-powered landing pages, campaign recommendations, product library enhancements, and universal campaigns.

The company has explained the functions of the tools as follows:

Universal Campaigns: One campaign that works across multiple ad formats with a single budget, optimizing in real-time for specific objectives like sales maximization.

AI-Powered Landing Pages: Creates shoppable brand destinations with AI to generate product showcases and custom copy.

Recommendations in Ads Manager: Gives data-driven insights that can be implemented with "a single click" to boost ad campaign performance.

Enhanced Product Library: Streamlined tools for managing product information at scale.

The AI-generated landing pages are available for all CPG advertisers, and other tools are currently in pilot with select brands, with plans for broader availability in the coming months, the company said.

The U.S. is the leading market for advertising, with ad spending expected to reach $455.9B by 2025, according to a report from Publift, in which digital ad spending is expected to see an 8-10% growth at about $325B this year.

data.title: HubSpot gets initiated with Market-Perform rating at Bernstein
data.description:
Bernstein began coverage of HubSpot (NYSE:HUBS) with a Market-Perform rating and a $693 price target.

Shares of HubSpot dipped about 1% on Thursday.

Analysts led by Firoz Valliji said HubSpot is a cloud-based sales and marketing software platform focused on business-to-business customers in the small and midsize businesses, or SMB, segment. "Over the years, HubSpot has done a good job of expanding its productportfolio, adding new customers, and up-selling to existing ones, resulting in best-in-classmarket share gain. In the foreseeable investment horizon, revenue growth will remain thekey thesis driver for the stock," the analysts added.

The analysts think HubSpot can deliver higher growth than Street expectations, as the company is well-positioned to continue gaining market share. Over the long term, the benefit of share gain will likely diminish as — customer relationship management, or CRM, is a maturing market; and HubSpot’s revenue base grows. 

Valliji and his team added that HubSpot will then need to deliver on profitability, which they think the company can. Overall, the analysts are optimistic about HubSpot's fundamental story. However, they said that valuation is at the higher end of historical levels and at a premium to software peers because the company is viewed as a beneficiary of potential SMB upswing and due to the shift in investor preference toward low-capex software vendors. Thus, the analysts think that the upside potential is capped.

data.title: J.P. Morgan sees opportunities in QSR sector, upgrades CAVA on growth potential
data.description:
Stock market volatility and the still-attractive 4.3% yield in the 10-year Treasury note has provided some investment opportunities in the quick service restaurant (“QSR”) industry. Coupled with upward revisions to comparable store sales for many names in the sector and limited exposure to changes in U.S. trade policies, J.P. Morgan singles out several names in the sector to outperform versus peers.

Among those is CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) which was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral with a nod to the stock’s 32% decline over the past 30 days.

“CAVA has significant US white space for expansion from its already multi-market success, is generating FCF unusually early, and has considerable near-term operational and brand initiatives to drive both sales and profits,” J.P. Morgan analysts John Ivankoe and Rahul Krotthapalli’ research showed.

CAVA’s growth trajectory is amplified by investments in technology to optimize labor and make the restaurants easier to run; a differentiated loyalty program; strong new store openings that are closing the “awareness gap” with peers like Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG); and new menu options including seafood. With these initiatives, J.P. Morgan sees CAVA’s (NYSE:CAVA) comparable store sales growing by 10% in Q1 and 7.2% in FY25, significantly more than Chipotle’s (CMG) much more modest +2.6% and +2.5%, respectively.

While Ivankoe and Krotthapalli maintain their neutral rating on Chipotle (CMG) and Brinker International (EAT), they would look to add to positions on further volatility. Chipotle (CMG) should see peak comparable sales in the second half of the year, with Q3 momentum “surprising” the pair as average unit volume (“AUV”) is expected to perform ahead of normal seasonality.

For the broader restaurant industry and economic landscape, Ivankoe and Krotthapalli pay particular attention to the expectations component of consumer confidence data, as a less optimistic consumer becomes more careful in their discretionary spending. According to the latest data compiled by the Conference Board, the February consumer expectations index dropped 9.3 points to 72.9. This is the first time since June 2024 the expectations index was below the threshold of 80 that signals a recession ahead.

On the labor front, talent sourcing and retention remain key for building strong brands and effectively scaling operations. The study shows that turnover trends are improving among those embracing increased technology but remain below scale for fast casual names that remain focused on attracting the “right” talent.

By demographics, food spending as a percentage of total spending is 15.6% for the bottom 20% of income consumers (4.7% at restaurants, 11% at grocery stores) and 11.3% for the top 20% (5.2% restaurants, 6.1% grocery). As Washington considers exemptions for some agricultural goods from tariffs on Mexico and Canada, its questionable as to tariffs’ effectiveness on food staples not able to be produced in the U.S. at a rate to sustain domestic consumption, especially as it would have disproportionate impact on the lower income consumer.

The upgrade is giving a boost to CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) with shares up 5.4%.
